2026-04-22T08:38:13.4148522Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4149061Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4149485Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4149844Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4150330Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4150566Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4150724Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4150901Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4151067Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4151294Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4151493Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4151776Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:38:13.4152029Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). 1.1.0-beta.2 (2023-11-27)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Enable mocking for extension methods, refer this [document](https://aka.ms/azsdk/net/mocking) for more details.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Fixed all `Azure.ResourceManager` to `1.4.0`.\n\n## 1.0.0 (2022-11-04)\n\nThis package is the first stable release of the Microsoft Azure Consumption management client library.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\nPolishing since last public beta release:\n- Prepended `Consumption` prefix to all single / simple model names.\n- Corrected the format of all `ResourceIdentifier` type properties / parameters.\n- Corrected the suffix of `DateTimeOffset` properties / parameters.\n- Introduced partial resource implementations for some methods to better support hierarchy structure.\n- Optimized the name of some models and functions.\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Upgraded dependent Azure.ResourceManager to 1.3.1.\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2022-08-29)\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\nNew design of track 2 initial commit.\n\n### Package Name\n\nThe package name has been changed from `Microsoft.Azure.Management.Consumption` to `Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption`.\n\n### General New Features\n\nThis package follows the [new Azure SDK guidelines](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/general_introduction.html), and provides many core capabilities:\n\n    - Support MSAL.NET, Azure.Identity is out of box for supporting MSAL.NET.\n    - Support [OpenTelemetry](https://opentelemetry.io/) for distributed tracing.\n    - HTTP pipeline with custom policies.\n    - Better error-handling.\n    - Support uniform telemetry across all languages.\n\nThis package is a Public Preview version, so expect incompatible changes in subsequent releases as we improve the product. Compute Fleet launches a combination of virtual machines (VMs) at the lowest price and highest capacity. There are many ways you can use this product, whether by running a stateless web service, a big data cluster, or a Continuous Integration pipeline.
